evals.name: batch rename across files
evals.prompt: A user asks: rename getUserById to findUser everywhere. It appears in 9 files and several tests. Describe the refactoring workflow before making changes.
evals.expected_output: The response treats this as batch refactoring, searches all affected locations first, uses MorphLLM edit_file or equivalent batch refactor flow instead of many manual edits, preserves behavior, batches edits per file, and verifies with tests.
assertions: The output recognizes this as a multi-file batch refactor rather than a simple single edit.
The output says to search or map all occurrences before editing.
The output mentions using MorphLLM edit_file or the skill's batch refactoring tool/workflow.
The output says behavior should be preserved unless the user explicitly requested behavior change.
The output says to batch all edits for the same file in one edit operation.
The output includes verification with relevant lint or tests after the refactor.

evals.name: high stakes batch refactor preview
evals.prompt: A user asks: replace duplicated payment validation scattered across services with a shared implementation. The change touches many files and is high stakes. Describe the refactoring workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response treats this as a behavior-preserving batch refactor, defines the preservation target, maps affected files before editing, previews high-stakes changes with dryRun or another reviewable preview, applies one coherent batch at a time, and verifies with tests.
assertions: The output states the behavior-preservation target before editing.
The output says to map affected files or occurrences before editing.
The output uses dryRun, preview mode, or another reviewable preview for the high-stakes change before applying edits.
The output applies one coherent batch at a time rather than mixing unrelated changes.
The output includes post-refactor lint/test verification.
